story_title: Ukraine-Russia war: bombardment, tribunal, and diplomacy
event_summary: Russia launched its heaviest wartime drone assault on Kyiv; 36 nations approved creation of a special international tribunal to prosecute Russian leadership; Ukraine and Russia exchanged 205 prisoners each; corruption probes touched Zelenskyy's office; and diplomacy advanced toward a new round of talks.
tldr: Five distinct headline frames compete across 25 buckets, with SECURITY_DEFENSE (bombardment/counterstrikes) dominating by volume — 13 buckets led with the drone assault or Ukrainian strikes. The tribunal story generated a tight LEGALITY cluster among European outlets. CRIME_PUNISHMENT covers a separate track: Ukraine corruption headlines from Iran opposition, South Africa, and wire services that imply institutional critique of Kyiv. The POLITICAL frame captures the Putin-China visit and Hungary post-Orbán speculation. The Nordic bucket is the most isolated — its headline covered Finland/Sweden roundup items semantically distant from the Kyiv-strike dominant frame. The Asia-Pacific regional bucket's counter-drone headline mentions no Ukraine-specific context, the clearest editorial avoidance in the corpus.

evidence.quote: Russia Unleashes Heaviest Wartime Drone Assault On Ukraine
Zelenskyy vows retribution after Russian strike on Kyiv
Russia considering plan to attack NATO country from Belarus
36 countries approve creation of special Ukraine tribunal
Dozens of European countries sign up to special tribunal
36 nations approve tribunal creation to prosecute Russia
Anti-Graft Prosecutor Seeks Detention Of Zelenskyy
What's at stake in the Andriy Yermak corruption probe
After Trump trip, Putin to visit China on May 19
Russia, Ukraine to agree on date of new round of talks
What a Post-Orbán Hungary Means for Hungarians and Europe
Ukraine and Russia swap 205 prisoners each
Russia and Ukraine swap 205 prisoners of war each
Ukrainian mother: 'My son was taken away by war'

isolation_top.note: Roundup headline naming Finnish/Swedish nationals; generic phrasing limits semantic alignment with the Kyiv-strike dominant frame.
Counter-drone headline mentions no Ukraine-specific context — the clearest editorial avoidance in the corpus.
Arms-dealer bust headline is geo-specific (Donbas-tied) but uses crime-framing orthogonal to the bombardment dominant frame.
First-person grief headline from Catholic News Agency diverges from military-metrics language across the rest of the corpus.
Japan Times headline focused on alliance commitments rather than Kyiv strike specifics; geopolitical-distance framing.

silences.what_they_covered_instead: Southeast Asia's counter-drone headline contained no Ukraine-specific frame — regional lens displaced the war story entirely.

One Ukraine-bucket headline covered reconstruction financing ('What Am I Missing?') — the rebuilding frame was absent from all other buckets and shows inward policy focus alongside the bombardment coverage.

bottom_line: SECURITY_DEFENSE dominated headline selection across 13 of 25 buckets, driven by Russia's record drone assault. The tribunal story produced a tight European LEGALITY cluster. The corruption-probe CRIME_PUNISHMENT headlines — concentrated in Iran opposition, South Africa, and wire services — represent the most geopolitically pointed counter-narrative against Kyiv in today's corpus.
